topics.title: Stress, Glucocorticoids, and Neuroendocrine Regulation: Impacts on Behavior and Memory
topics.description: This cluster explores the impact of stress and glucocorticoids on neuroendocrine function, focusing on the HPA axis and its effects on behavior, memory, anxiety, and depression. Studies investigate the molecular and hormonal pathways involved, emphasizing the role of corticosterone and its influence on hippocampal dynamics.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, specifically on how stress and glucocorticoid hormones affect various psychological and cognitive processes.

topics.title: Pathophysiology and Pharmacoresistance in Temporal Lobe Epilepsy: Insights from Rodent Models
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the mechanisms of temporal lobe epilepsy (TLE), emphasizing the role of status epilepticus (SE) in epileptogenesis and treatment-resistant epilepsy. Rodent models, such as kainic acid and pilocarpine-induced epilepsy, are utilized to explore themes of altered neuronal excitability, neurogenesis, and GABAergic transmission.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, particularly the study of mechanisms underlying temporal lobe epilepsy and pharmacoresistance.

topics.title: Functional Connectivity and Network Dynamics in Preclinical and Clinical Alzheimer's Disease
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the alterations in brain network dynamics, particularly the default mode network and other large-scale networks, as markers for cognitive decline in Alzheimer's disease and mild cognitive impairment. Methodologies include advanced imaging techniques like functional connectivity MRI to explore structural and functional disruptions.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Cocaine Addiction and Neural Adaptations: Mechanisms of Relapse and Sensitization
topics.description: This cluster explores the neuroplastic changes induced by cocaine, focusing on the dopamine system's role in reinforcement and relapse. It investigates cocaine-induced behavioral sensitization, adaptive neurophysiological and molecular changes, and the implications of these adaptations in persistent drug-seeking and relapse.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of cocaine addiction and relapse mechanisms.

topics.title: Dynamic Functional Connectivity in Resting-State fMRI: Analyzing Intrinsic Connectivity Networks and Brain Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the characterization and analysis of dynamic functional connectivity in resting-state fMRI to understand intrinsic connectivity networks and brain network dynamics. It employs methodologies like independent component analysis to identify functional networks and explore temporal fluctuations and states within brain connectivity patterns.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of brain network dynamics and intrinsic connectivity networks.

topics.title: Neural Dynamics and Synchrony in Cortical Circuits
topics.description: This research cluster explores the mechanisms and implications of neural synchrony and spike train correlations in cortical networks, with a focus on how synaptic dynamics and spatiotemporal patterns contribute to information processing and variability in neuronal activity.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of neural synchrony and variability in cortical networks.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Reward Valuation and Decision Making
topics.description: This research cluster explores the neural encoding of reward valuation and decision-making processes, focusing on the role of different prefrontal cortex regions and their involvement in intertemporal choice and reinforcement learning.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the thematic exploration of neural mechanisms underlying reward-based decision making and valuation.

topics.title: Neuroprotective Strategies Targeting Oxidative Stress and Apoptosis in Ischemic and Neurodegenerative Disorders
topics.description: This research cluster explores neuroprotective approaches targeting oxidative stress and apoptosis involving antioxidants, anti-inflammatory agents, and novel compounds to mitigate neuronal damage in ischemic and neurodegenerative conditions.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies applied to achieve neuroprotection against oxidative stress and apoptosis.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms in Action and Cognition: Bridging Motor Behavior and Cognitive Processes
topics.description: This research cluster investigates the interplay between motor behavior and cognitive processes, focusing on neural mechanisms underlying action observation, motor task performance, and the overlapping regions in the brain involved in both motor and cognitive functions. This research cluster explores the roles of the motor and premotor cortices, as well as the mirror neuron system. Key methodologies include fMRI, TMS, and neural recording techniques to dissect the processing of motor control and sensorimotor integration.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, particularly the investigation of neural activity patterns during grasping and action observation through advanced imaging and stimulation techniques.

topics.title: The Role of Estrogen and Androgen Derivatives in Brain Function and Behavior
topics.description: This cluster explores the complex roles of estrogen and its metabolites on brain function, cognition, and sexual behavior, emphasizing the dynamic interactions between classical and non-genomic signaling pathways, as well as rapid modulation of estrogen synthesis and receptor activation in different brain regions.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, specifically the roles of estrogen and its derivatives in regulating brain function and behavior.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Sentence and Lexical Processing
topics.description: This cluster investigates the neural dynamics of language processing, focusing on how the brain comprehends sentences, resolves semantic ambiguity, and accesses lexical information. Studies utilize various methodologies such as fMRI and ERP to explore the distinct roles of brain regions, including the left inferior frontal gyrus and temporal lobe, in different linguistic tasks.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of sentence and lexical processing.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Speech Perception and Attention in Complex Listening Environments
topics.description: This cluster investigates the mechanisms underlying speech perception and auditory processing, emphasizing neural responses to auditory stimuli and their integration with visual information. Key themes include event-related potentials such as N400 and MMN, as well as the role of neural oscillations and cognitive load in modulating speech intelligibility and auditory stream segregation.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of neural processing and multisensory integration in speech perception.

topics.title: Functional and Network Dysconnectivity in Schizophrenia: Insights into Cognitive Deficits
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the dysconnectivity of brain networks, particularly the default mode network, in schizophrenia, leading to cognitive deficits in working memory and executive function. The studies employ methodologies such as resting-state fMRI and dynamic functional connectivity analyses to investigate disruptions in neural circuits related to symptoms and cognitive impairments.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the thematic exploration of dysconnectivity in brain networks affecting cognitive functions in schizophrenia.

topics.title: Pathogenesis and Therapeutic Mechanisms in Multiple Sclerosis
topics.description: This cluster of research focuses on the immunopathogenetic mechanisms of multiple sclerosis, emphasizing the roles of B cells, T cells, and the central nervous system immune environment. It consolidates developments in therapeutic strategies targeting these immune components to mitigate disease progression.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of autoimmune mechanisms and immune system interactions within the central nervous system in multiple sclerosis.

topics.title: Neural Modulation and Plasticity through Non-Invasive Brain Stimulation: Enhancements in Motor and Cognitive Functions
topics.description: This research cluster explores the modulation of neural excitability and plasticity using transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS) and transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS), examining their effects on motor performance and cognitive enhancement in both healthy individuals and those with neurological impairments.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies involved in non-invasive brain stimulation techniques and their applications in enhancing motor and cognitive functions.

topics.title: Mechanisms of Leptin Resistance and Central Regulation of Feeding Behavior in Diet-Induced Obesity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on understanding the mechanisms of leptin resistance and its implications for feeding behavior in the context of diet-induced obesity. It examines both the neural and hormonal regulation within the hypothalamus, including the roles of neuropeptides such as NPY and POMC in modulating energy balance and food intake.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of hormonal and neural regulation of feeding behavior in the context of leptin resistance and diet-induced obesity.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Emotion Processing and Regulation
topics.description: This research cluster explores the neural underpinnings of emotion processing and regulation, utilizing a variety of neuroimaging and electrophysiological methods to investigate automatic and controlled emotional responses. Key themes include the roles of the amygdala and prefrontal cortex in processing emotional valence and arousal, as well as the modulation of cognitive control and attention by emotional stimuli.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, specifically the use of multimodal neuroimaging techniques to understand the temporal and spatial dynamics of emotion processing and regulation.

topics.title: Mechanisms and Outcomes of Auditory Damage and Repair
topics.description: The cluster explores the impact of noise exposure and ototoxic substances on auditory hair cells and synapses within the cochlea, addressing both the mechanisms of damage and potential pathways for cellular repair and hearing restoration.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Mechanisms and Neural Substrates of Fear Extinction and Conditioning
topics.description: The cluster explores the neural circuits and molecular mechanisms involved in fear extinction and conditioning, focusing on the roles of the amygdala, prefrontal cortex, and hippocampus in memory consolidation and synaptic plasticity, as well as various fear conditioning paradigms including contextual and trace fear conditioning.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies and neural mechanisms employed in studying fear conditioning and extinction.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Attention and Working Memory: Processes and Interactions
topics.description: This cluster examines the interaction between attention and working memory, focusing on how both top-down and stimulus-driven processes modulate neural activity across cortical regions primarily within the frontoparietal network. Techniques such as ERP and fMRI are utilized to explore attentional modulation and control in visual processing and memory tasks.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies used to investigate the neural processes underlying attention and working memory interactions, particularly through various imaging and electrophysiological techniques.

topics.title: Olfactory Coding and Mixture Interactions in Sensory Processing
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the olfactory system's mechanisms for encoding odor information, exploring how odorant receptors and glomerular activity contribute to the perception and discrimination of odors, including complex mixtures, and addressing challenges like concentration invariance and temporal dynamics in olfactory coding.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on both the themes and methodologies, with a particular emphasis on uncovering mechanistic insights and theoretical frameworks for processing olfactory information.

topics.title: Dynamics of Oculomotor Control: Saccadic and Pursuit Eye Movements in Visual Processing
topics.description: This research cluster explores the intricate interplay between saccadic and smooth pursuit eye movements in maintaining visual stability and processing visual information. It highlights the neural mechanisms underlying eye movements, perisaccadic mislocalization, and the role of visual attention in oculomotor control.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, particularly the development and application of models and experiments to investigate the neural and behavioral mechanisms of eye movements.

topics.title: Impact of Adolescent Binge Drinking on Neurocognitive Development and Vulnerability to Alcohol Use Disorders
topics.description: This research cluster explores the effects of adolescent binge drinking on brain development and behavior in rodent models, emphasizing changes in cognitive flexibility, neurogenesis, and prefrontal cortex activity, which contribute to increased vulnerability to alcohol use disorders.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Spatial Memory and Navigation: Dynamics of Place Cells and Hippocampal Networks
topics.description: The research cluster focuses on the role of hippocampal networks, particularly place cells in regions CA1 and CA3, in spatial navigation and memory formation. It explores the dynamic remapping of place cells, their interaction with grid cells and head direction cells, and how these elements contribute to constructing a cognitive map for both spatial and episodic memory tasks.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of spatial navigation and memory representation.

topics.title: Dynamics of Attention and Memory in Visual Search
topics.description: This research cluster investigates mechanisms of visual search and attentional guidance, focusing on how attentional templates, feature-based and object-based selection, and contextual cueing influence search efficiency and distractor suppression. The studies analyze both the temporal dynamics of attentional shifts and the role of learned regularities in modulating attentional focus.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of visual search mechanisms and attentional strategies.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Episodic Memory Across the Lifespan
topics.description: This research cluster examines the neural underpinnings of episodic memory, focusing on age-related changes, the role of the hippocampus and cortical regions, and the differentiation between recollection and familiarity processes. The studies employ methodologies such as fMRI, ERP, and multivoxel pattern analysis to investigate these mechanisms.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of episodic memory processes and aging.

topics.title: Neurobiological Mechanisms and Connectivity Patterns in Autism Spectrum Disorder
topics.description: This cluster explores the neurobiological underpinnings and connectivity disruptions in autism spectrum disorder, with a focus on functional and structural brain networks, genetic influences, and behavioral symptoms. It highlights the heterogeneity in ASD and employs a range of methodologies including resting-state functional MRI, EEG, and animal models.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of autism spectrum disorder and related neurobiological and behavioral analyses.

topics.title: Visuomotor Adaptation and Motor Learning: Mechanisms and Coordination Strategies
topics.description: This research cluster explores the adaptation of reaching and grasping movements with a focus on visuomotor adaptation, feedback control mechanisms, sensorimotor integration, and anticipatory control strategies in response to perturbations and interaction with environmental dynamics.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies for understanding and improving motor control, particularly through the development and application of techniques for feedback control and adaptation.

topics.title: Neurogenesis and Cortical Development: Role of Signaling Pathways and Progenitor Dynamics
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the role of signaling pathways, such as Wnt-β-catenin, and the dynamics of neural progenitor cells in the regulation of neurogenesis and cortical development. It highlights the interplay between cell cycle regulation and signaling cues that govern the proliferation, differentiation, and migration of progenitor cells during brain development.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of neurogenesis and cortical development.

topics.title: Mechanisms and Dynamics of Axon Guidance and Neuronal Polarization
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the mechanisms and dynamics involved in axon guidance and neuronal polarization, emphasizing cytoskeletal rearrangements, signaling pathways such as those mediated by semaphorins and netrins, and the roles of Rho GTPases in orchestrating growth cone behavior and neurite outgrowth.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies for studying the signaling pathways and cytoskeletal dynamics that facilitate neurite outgrowth and guidance.

topics.title: Nicotinic Acetylcholine Receptors: Mechanisms of Addiction and Neuropharmacological Insights
topics.description: The cluster examines the role of nicotinic acetylcholine receptors (nAChRs) in nicotine addiction, focusing on the α4β2 and α7 subtypes. Research explores receptor desensitization, upregulation, and the neuroprotective effects of nicotine, with implications for cognitive enhancement and addiction therapy.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, particularly the study of nAChR subtypes' roles in nicotine addiction and their potential as therapeutic targets for neuroprotection and cognitive enhancement.

topics.title: Schizophrenia Research: NMDA Receptor Hypofunction and Dopaminergic Dysregulation
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the pathophysiology of schizophrenia with emphasis on NMDA receptor hypofunction and dopaminergic dysregulation as critical mechanisms in its onset and progression. The methodologies predominantly involve animal models to study the effectiveness of antipsychotics and their impact on cognitive and social behavioral deficits related to schizophrenia.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the thematic exploration of schizophrenia pathophysiology, particularly involving NMDA receptor hypofunction and dopamine system dysregulation.

topics.title: Advanced Neuroimaging Techniques for Characterization and Automation of Brain Anatomy Using Diffusion MRI
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the development and application of state-of-the-art diffusion MRI technologies to produce detailed brain atlases and templates that aid in understanding brain microstructure, connectivity, and anatomical variability across species. The research particularly emphasizes automated segmentation techniques, tractography for white matter pathways, and the creation of atlases for specific species such as non-human primates.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, specifically on method development for enhanced imaging, segmentation, and interpretation of brain structures.

topics.title: Investigation of Serotonin Receptors and Adaptations in Antidepressant Mechanisms
topics.description: The research cluster focuses on the role of serotonin (5-HT) receptors, particularly 5-HT1A, 5-HT2A, and 5-HT2C, in the pathophysiology of depression and the adaptive mechanisms following antidepressant treatments, emphasizing the neurochemical adaptations in the raphe nuclei and hippocampal neurogenesis.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Pain Network Dynamics in Chronic and Experimental Pain Studies
topics.description: This cluster focuses on understanding the neural basis of chronic and experimental pain through brain imaging and functional connectivity studies, examining modulation systems and neural adaptations in the context of pain perception and management.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies used, emphasizing brain imaging and functional connectivity techniques to explore neural dynamics in pain.

topics.title: Retinal Neurodegeneration and Protection: Exploring Mechanisms, Targets, and Interventions
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the pathogenetic mechanisms and therapeutic interventions for retinal neurodegenerative diseases such as glaucoma and age-related macular degeneration. It highlights the roles of retinal ganglion cells and photoreceptors in degeneration, emphasizing the potential of neuroprotective strategies involving factors like RIPK1, GDF15, VEGF, and the employment of electroretinograms for evaluation.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, specifically the mechanisms of retinal degeneration and potential therapeutic pathways.

topics.title: Complex Processing Mechanisms in Sound Localization and Auditory Information Integration
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the neural mechanisms of sound localization, examining the roles of frequency tuning, spatial selectivity, and diverse synaptic interactions in the inferior colliculus and auditory cortex across species. Studies address corticofugal modulation and its effects on auditory processing, with emphasis on frequency modulation and the spatial-temporal integration of complex sound stimuli.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, specifically the study of neural processing of auditory information and sound localization.

topics.title: Tau Pathology Mechanisms and Therapeutic Strategies in Alzheimer's Disease and Tauopathies
topics.description: The cluster focuses on the pathophysiological mechanisms involving tau protein modifications, particularly hyperphosphorylation, truncation, and aggregation in Alzheimer's disease and related tauopathies, exploring the propagation of tau pathology and emerging therapeutic strategies.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the thematic exploration of tau-related pathophysiological mechanisms in neurodegenerative diseases.

topics.title: Diversity and Specialization in Corticothalamic and Pyramidal Neurons: Anatomical Connectivity and Functional Implications
topics.description: This research cluster explores the diversity and specialization of corticothalamic and pyramidal neurons in the cerebral cortex, examining their anatomical connectivity, synaptic projections, and functional roles in sensory processing and intracortical communication.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, particularly in understanding the anatomical connectivity and synaptic interactions of specific neuron types.

topics.title: Mechanisms of Myelination and Remyelination in CNS: Insights from Oligodendrocyte Biology
topics.description: This cluster explores the cellular and molecular mechanisms underlying oligodendrocyte differentiation and myelination in the central nervous system, with a focus on remyelination in diseases like multiple sclerosis. It addresses growth factors, the role of oligodendrocyte progenitor cells (OPCs), and intracellular signal pathways involved in these processes.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, particularly on the development and application of in vitro and in vivo models to study myelination and remyelination mechanisms, along with myelin-related cellular interactions and signaling pathways.

topics.title: Visual Cortex Mechanisms in Object Recognition and Scene Segmentation
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the mechanisms by which the visual cortex processes visual information, emphasizing receptive field dynamics, orientation selectivity, and the integration of local features into global percepts for object recognition and scene segmentation. The studies examine the roles of both feedforward and feedback signals across different areas like V1 and V2 in enhancing the perception of complex scenes.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of understanding how visual cortex mechanisms contribute to object recognition and scene segmentation.

topics.title: Protein Misfolding and Propagation in Parkinson's Disease and Synucleinopathies
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the role of alpha-synuclein aggregation and the formation of Lewy bodies as central events in the development of Parkinson's disease and related synucleinopathies, with emphasis on prion-like propagation and the molecular mechanisms underlying neurodegeneration.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, specifically the study of alpha-synuclein aggregation and propagation as key elements in the pathogenesis of synucleinopathies.

topics.title: Cannabinoid Receptors and Endocannabinoid System Impact on Neural Function and Plasticity
topics.description: This cluster explores the roles of cannabinoid receptors and the endocannabinoid system in regulating synaptic transmission, learning, memory, and neuroplasticity, highlighting both the therapeutic potential and cognitive effects of cannabinoid compounds.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Functional Connectivity and Network Dysregulation in Depression: Insights from Resting-State fMRI
topics.description: This research cluster explores the implications of altered functional connectivity and network activity in major depressive disorder, examining disruptions within key networks such as the default mode network, salience network, and cognitive control network.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Explorations in GABAergic Synaptic and Extrasynaptic Dynamics: Receptor Composition, Function, and Inhibition
topics.description: This research cluster examines the molecular composition and functional roles of GABA(A) and GABA(B) receptors in mediating synaptic and extrasynaptic inhibition within the CNS, focusing on the modulation of neuronal excitability through phasic and tonic currents and the pharmacological implications of different receptor subtypes.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of GABAergic neurotransmission and its implications for neuronal excitability and inhibition.

topics.title: Exploration of Mechanisms Underlying L-DOPA-Induced Dyskinesia in Parkinson's Disease Treatment
topics.description: This cluster of research investigates the mechanisms and pathways involved in L-DOPA-induced dyskinesia, focusing on the role of dopamine receptors, GABAergic and serotonergic systems, and neuroplasticity in Parkinson's disease models.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of L-DOPA-induced dyskinesia and its underlying mechanisms.

topics.title: Sleep's Role in Memory Consolidation and Cognitive Function
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the role of sleep stages, particularly REM and NREM sleep, in memory consolidation and cognitive function, examining EEG patterns, sleep spindles, and homeostatic regulation associated with sleep quality and deprivation effects.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Neuroplasticity and Functional Reorganization in Rehabilitation After Stroke and Spinal Cord Injury
topics.description: The cluster focuses on neuroplasticity-driven motor recovery and functional reorganization after stroke and spinal cord injury, highlighting the role of rehabilitation therapies in promoting cortical and spinal pathway adaptations to improve motor function.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of neuroplasticity and functional reorganization in motor recovery after injury.

topics.title: Apolipoprotein E and Its Role in Alzheimer's Disease Pathogenesis and Cholesterol Metabolism
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the role of Apolipoprotein E (APOE) gene polymorphisms, particularly the ε4 allele, in the pathogenesis of Alzheimer's disease, exploring its effects on cholesterol metabolism, amyloid-beta deposition, and cognitive decline.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes related to Alzheimer's disease pathogenesis and genetic risks.

topics.title: Advancements in Optogenetic Tools and Neural Probes for In Vivo Brain Function Studies
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the development and application of advanced optogenetic tools and neural probes for studying brain function in vivo, emphasizing chronic implants, optical stimulation, and high-resolution electrophysiology in freely moving subjects. The cluster highlights innovations in μLED technology, open-source device fabrication, and the integration of multi-modality approaches for precise neural modulation and recording.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies employed for neural recording and modulation, utilizing advanced optogenetic systems and innovative designs for chronic implantable devices.

topics.title: Innovative Approaches to Treating Treatment-Resistant Depression through Modulation of Neuroplasticity and Inflammation
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on developing novel treatment strategies for major depressive disorder and treatment-resistant depression by targeting neuroplasticity and inflammatory pathways. Studies frequently utilize animal models to assess the efficacy of various compounds in reversing depression-like behaviors through modulation of brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) and mTOR signaling pathways, primarily in the hippocampus.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, particularly targeting neuroplasticity and inflammatory pathways to address depression.

topics.title: Theta-Gamma Oscillatory Dynamics in Memory and Cognitive Processing
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the role of theta and gamma oscillations in the cognitive processes of memory encoding and retrieval, underlining the importance of cross-frequency coupling and phase opposition in the hippocampus. Electrophysiological recording methods highlight how these oscillations coordinate neural activity and influence behavioral performance.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of theta and gamma oscillations as they relate to cognitive processes such as memory and attention.

topics.title: Circadian Rhythm Mechanisms and Influencing Factors
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the mechanisms of circadian rhythms, highlighting the role of the suprachiasmatic nucleus as the master clock, and the impact of light, melatonin, and various genetic and environmental factors on clock gene expression and synchronization of biological clocks.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes related to circadian rhythms and their modulation by different environmental and genetic factors.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Social Cognition and Moral Judgment
topics.description: This research cluster explores the neural underpinnings of social cognition and moral judgment, emphasizing Theory of Mind and empathy as central themes. It investigates how different brain regions, particularly the medial prefrontal cortex and temporoparietal junction, contribute to understanding others' mental states and making moral evaluations.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of social cognition and moral judgment.

topics.title: Neuroendocrine and Autonomic Mechanisms in Cardiovascular Regulation and Hypertension
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the neuroendocrine and autonomic mechanisms underlying blood pressure regulation and hypertension, emphasizing the roles of angiotensin II, catecholamines, and the sympathetic nervous system in modulating baroreflex and cardiovascular function through central and peripheral pathways.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Neuroinflammation and Immune Dynamics in Alzheimer's Disease
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the neuroinflammatory processes involved in Alzheimer's disease, highlighting the roles of microglia and astrocytes in mediating immune responses to amyloid-beta deposition and neurodegeneration.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of inflammatory mechanisms and immune system interactions in Alzheimer's disease.

topics.title: Neuroimmune Modulation in Stress-Induced Anxiety and Neuroinflammation
topics.description: This cluster explores the impact of innate immune stimulation on preventing anxiety-like behaviors induced by chronic stress, focusing on the role of microglial activation, pro-inflammatory cytokines, and neuroinflammatory responses in the hippocampus and prefrontal cortex.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on methodologies, particularly the consistent use of immune stimulation techniques to explore stress-induced neuroinflammation and anxiety-like behaviors.

topics.title: Neural Modulation and Therapeutic Mechanisms in Deep Brain Stimulation for Parkinson's Disease
topics.description: The research cluster focuses on the neural modulation achieved through deep brain stimulation (DBS) of the subthalamic nucleus (STN) and globus pallidus internus (GPi) for relieving motor symptoms in Parkinson's disease. The main themes involve understanding the effects of high-frequency stimulation on beta oscillations and exploring adaptive DBS mechanisms for optimizing therapeutic outcomes.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, specifically the development and application of DBS parameters and adaptive systems for therapeutic effectiveness.

topics.title: Oxytocin's Role in Social Behavior and Bonding
topics.description: This cluster explores the role of oxytocin in regulating social behaviors, including partner preference, parental care, and social bonding across species, highlighting the neuropeptide's effects on neural pathways and stress modulation.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes related to social behavior and bonding mediated by oxytocin.

topics.title: Vestibular System Functionality and Its Influence on Spatial Orientation and Balance Control
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the complex interactions between otolith organs, semicircular canals, and central neural processes that facilitate vestibulo-ocular and vestibulospinal reflexes, spatial orientation, and self-motion perception. It highlights how the vestibular system integrates multisensory inputs to maintain balance and orientation, with a thematic emphasis on understanding vestibular contributions to posture and perception in varying gravitational and sensory environments.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes regarding the vestibular system's roles in spatial orientation and balance.

topics.title: Neural and Psychophysical Insights into Motion Perception Mechanisms
topics.description: This cluster explores the mechanisms through which the visual system processes motion, integrating both local and global motion signals. Key themes include resolving the aperture problem, understanding motion aftereffects, and the roles of different cortical areas like MT and MST in motion processing. Methods include psychophysical experiments and neural recordings, emphasizing the interplay between first-order and second-order motion processing.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of motion perception mechanisms and how the visual system integrates motion signals to interpret complex motion patterns.

topics.title: Dopamine Signaling in Reinforcement Learning and Motivational Processes
topics.description: The cluster focuses on the role of dopamine in reinforcement learning and motivational processes, particularly on how dopamine modulates reward prediction errors and influences action selection and behavioral adaptation.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, particularly approaches that explore dopamine's role in computational models of learning and motivation.

topics.title: Developmental Trajectories in Adolescent Brain Functional and Structural Connectivity
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the characterization of brain development during childhood and adolescence, emphasizing the maturation of structural and functional brain networks. Utilizing advanced neuroimaging techniques such as diffusion tensor imaging and functional MRI, studies explore the relationship between brain connectivity and cognitive development, highlighting the role of white matter changes and cortical thickness across critical developmental periods.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of brain development and connectivity during adolescence.

topics.title: Multisensory Integration and Tactile Perception: Explorations in Tactile, Visual, and Cross-modal Interactions
topics.description: This research cluster explores the neural basis and cognitive mechanisms of tactile perception, focusing on multisensory integration involving tactile, visual, and haptic cues. It examines how these sensory modalities interact and affect spatial localization, body schema, and perception accuracy, utilizing psychophysical, neuroimaging, and cross-modal paradigms.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of multisensory integration and tactile perception.

topics.title: Multifaceted Assessment of Motor and Cognitive Aspects in Parkinson's Disease
topics.description: This research cluster explores the complex interplay between motor and cognitive functions in Parkinson's disease. It emphasizes the impact of dopaminergic medication on executive dysfunction and motor symptoms, investigates gait variability and freezing of gait, examines the effects of dual tasking, and addresses both non-motor symptoms such as apathy and speech abnormalities.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of motor and cognitive symptomatology in Parkinson's disease.

topics.title: Biomarkers and Neuroimaging in Alzheimer's Disease: Pathophysiology and Diagnostic Advances
topics.description: This cluster explores the use of cerebrospinal fluid biomarkers and neuroimaging techniques to advance the diagnostic accuracy and understanding of Alzheimer's disease pathophysiology, focusing on the detection and progression from preclinical to clinical stages.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies.

topics.title: Neuroimaging and Connectivity Analysis in Epilepsy: Mapping the Epileptogenic Network
topics.description: This cluster investigates the spatial and temporal dynamics of epileptic networks using advanced neuroimaging and EEG methods, including fMRI, MEG, and iEEG. It focuses on identifying and characterizing epileptogenic zones and understanding seizure propagation and network alterations.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies employed to investigate and map the epileptogenic networks associated with different types of epilepsy.

topics.title: Synergistic Muscle Activation and Control Mechanisms in Biomechanics and Human Locomotion
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on how muscle synergies facilitate the coordination of complex movements and balance control in humans. Key methodologies include electromyography for muscle activation analysis and system identification for reflex modulation during tasks such as walking and balance maintenance.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of muscle coordination and synergy in different biomechanical contexts, rather than specific methodologies.

topics.title: Diabetes and Cognitive Dysfunction: Linking Metabolic Disorders and Alzheimer's Pathology
topics.description: This research cluster explores the connection between diabetes, particularly insulin resistance, and Alzheimer's disease, emphasizing impaired insulin signaling and its role in cognitive deficits. Key methodologies include in vitro and in vivo models to study the impact of hyperglycemia and diabetes-related metabolic disturbances on brain function and neurodegeneration.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of insulin resistance and its impact on Alzheimer's pathology and cognitive impairment.

topics.title: Molecular and Genetic Underpinnings of Psychiatric Disorders: A Focus on Schizophrenia and Bipolar Disorder
topics.description: This cluster centers on the intricate genetic and epigenetic mechanisms that contribute to schizophrenia and bipolar disorder, emphasizing the role of genetic variants, DNA methylation, and gene-environment interactions in shaping neurodevelopmental and synaptic pathways.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the genetic and epigenetic themes of psychiatric disorders, specifically their influence on schizophrenia and bipolar disorder pathogenesis.

topics.title: Neurobiological and Cognitive Mechanisms of ADHD: Insights from Genetics and Neuroimaging
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the neurobiological underpinnings and cognitive deficits of ADHD, emphasizing its genetic basis, dysfunctional neural circuits involving the dopamine system, and altered brain network connectivity. Studies investigate both child and adult populations, utilizing neuroimaging techniques to explore structural and functional brain differences, and examine the effects of interventions like pharmacotherapy.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Mitochondrial Dynamics and Mitophagy in Neurodegeneration
topics.description: This research cluster explores the critical role of mitochondria in neuronal function and the pathogenesis of neurodegenerative diseases, focusing on the processes of mitochondrial dynamics, including fission and fusion, and the selective degradation of mitochondria through mitophagy.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of neurodegenerative disease mechanisms involving mitochondrial dynamics and dysfunction.

topics.title: Advancements in EEG-based Brain-Computer Interfaces for Neuroprosthetic Control
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the development and application of EEG-based brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) for neuroprosthetic control, emphasizing methodologies like motor imagery, signal processing, and the use of deep learning algorithms to improve system accuracy and user experience.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies.

topics.title: Exploring Hemodynamic Modulations and Neurovascular Coupling in fMRI Studies
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the exploration of hemodynamic changes and neurovascular coupling through various fMRI techniques, emphasizing the measurement and calibration of blood oxygenation level-dependent (BOLD) responses and cerebral blood flow (CBF). It evaluates the utilization of calibrated fMRI, resting state fluctuation amplitude (RSFA), and variations in vascular reactivity for accurate characterization of cognitive and resting states in brain function.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, including hemodynamic response evaluation and fMRI calibration techniques, to enhance the understanding of the physiological basis of BOLD signals.

topics.title: Neurocognitive Connectivity and Dysfunction in PTSD Across Age Groups
topics.description: The cluster studies the alterations in brain network connectivity related to PTSD, with a focus on the default mode, salience, and executive networks, exploring differences between adolescents and adults. Resting-state fMRI and seed-based connectivity analyses are employed to investigate the neural correlates of trauma exposure, PTSD symptoms, and their impact on cognitive function.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of understanding PTSD and its impact on brain connectivity and cognitive functioning.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms and Cognitive Processes in Musical Syntax and Tonal Hierarchy
topics.description: This research cluster investigates the neural correlates and cognitive mechanisms underlying the perception and processing of musical syntax and tonal hierarchy, focusing on how musical training and congenital amusia affect auditory processing and pitch perception.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies employed to study musical syntax, tonal hierarchy, and auditory processing in both trained musicians and individuals with congenital amusia.

topics.title: Hypothermia and Neuroprotection in Neonatal Hypoxia-Ischemia: Mechanisms and Therapeutic Implications
topics.description: The research cluster focuses on understanding the mechanisms and therapeutic potential of hypothermia in providing neuroprotection against neonatal hypoxic-ischemic brain injury. Key themes include the role of hypothermia in reducing apoptosis, modulating inflammation, and preserving neural stem cells, along with exploring the cognitive impacts of intermittent hypoxia.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies of hypothermia and its neuroprotective effects.

topics.title: Gray Matter Pathology and Cognitive Impairment in Multiple Sclerosis
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the investigation of gray matter atrophy, cognitive impairment, and the neurodegenerative processes in multiple sclerosis, with extensive use of advanced MRI techniques to study structural and functional brain changes.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Advancements and Techniques in Acute Stroke Management
topics.description: This cluster focuses on recent advancements and methodologies in the treatment of acute ischemic and hemorrhagic strokes, emphasizing the efficacy of mechanical thrombectomy, thrombolysis, and endovascular treatments in optimizing recanalization and improving patient outcomes.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on methodologies.

topics.title: The Multifaceted Roles of Nitric Oxide in Neuronal Function and Neurogenesis
topics.description: This research cluster explores the diverse roles of nitric oxide (NO) in the brain, focusing on its involvement in neurotransmission, neurogenesis, synaptic plasticity, and memory processes. NO synthase (NOS) is a critical enzyme in these processes, with its inhibitors and pathways being key study areas.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, particularly the multifaceted roles and implications of nitric oxide in various neurological processes.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Face Perception and Recognition
topics.description: This cluster explores the neural mechanisms involved in face perception and recognition, emphasizing the role of face-selective regions such as the fusiform face area (FFA) and the occipital face area (OFA). Studies focus on neural adaptations and the face inversion effect, mainly using EEG and ERP methodologies, to understand holistic and part-based processing of facial features.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies used to study the neural processes underlying face perception and recognition.

topics.title: Effects of Physical Exercise on Neurogenesis and Cognitive Function
topics.description: This research cluster evaluates the influence of physical exercise on cognitive function and brain health, focusing on hippocampal neurogenesis and the role of BDNF as a mediator of these effects, particularly in aging and Alzheimer’s disease contexts.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes related to physical exercise's role in promoting cognitive health and neurogenesis.

topics.title: Neural Dynamics of Anxiety: Anticipation, Connectivity, and Emotional Processing
topics.description: This cluster examines the neural mechanisms underlying anxiety, focusing on anticipatory processes, emotional response, and altered brain connectivity, particularly involving the amygdala and prefrontal cortex, using fMRI and EEG methodologies.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, particularly the use of neuroimaging techniques to explore the neural underpinnings of anxiety-related processes.

topics.title: Neurodegenerative Mechanisms and Therapeutic Strategies in Huntington's Disease
topics.description: The research cluster explores the mechanisms and therapeutic strategies for Huntington's disease, focusing on the pathogenic role of CAG repeat expansions, proteostasis, and transcriptional dysregulation, with an emphasis on animal models to evaluate potential interventions.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes related to the pathogenesis and therapeutic interventions of Huntington's disease.

topics.title: Cochlear Implants and Auditory Processing: Understanding Speech Perception and Integration
topics.description: This cluster explores cochlear implants focusing on auditory processing and speech perception, examining neural encoding and the integration of temporal cues for improved hearing rehabilitation.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on both themes and methodologies, with a strong emphasis on understanding cochlear implants' impact on auditory processing and speech perception.

topics.title: Microbiota-Gut-Brain Axis in Inflammatory and Neurological Disorders
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the bidirectional communication between the gut microbiota and the central nervous system, exploring how alterations in the microbiota-gut-brain axis influence inflammation, neurological development, and behavior. It highlights the therapeutic potential of targeting gut microbiota to alleviate symptoms of disorders such as inflammatory bowel disease and mental health conditions.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Neuroplasticity and Cognitive Mechanisms in Aphasia Post-Stroke Recovery
topics.description: This cluster examines the neural and cognitive mechanisms underlying language recovery in aphasia after stroke, focusing on neuroplastic changes and the reorganization of language functions across brain hemispheres, utilizing various neuroimaging techniques and analyzing the impact of specific cortical and subcortical brain regions on semantic and phonological processing.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of language recovery and neuroplasticity in aphasia following stroke.

topics.title: Astrocytic Metabolism and Neuronal Energy Dynamics in Brain Activation
topics.description: The abstracts characterize astrocyte-mediated energy metabolism in the brain, emphasizing the role of glycolysis, lactate shuttle, and glycogenolysis in meeting neuronal energy demands during activation. The studies highlight the glutamate-glutamine cycle, the significance of pyruvate as a metabolic intermediary, and the differential use of oxidative versus anaerobic glycolysis in neurons and astrocytes.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms and Cognitive Implications of Anesthetics
topics.description: This cluster examines the effects of anesthetics like isoflurane and sevoflurane on brain function, highlighting changes in neural connectivity, EEG patterns, and potential cognitive impairments. The research emphasizes the dose-dependent impact on network dynamics and explores mechanisms across different anesthetic agents.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, particularly the systematic investigation of anesthetic effects on brain connectivity and function.

topics.title: Morphological and Functional Dynamics of Dendritic Spines in Synaptic Plasticity and Neurodevelopmental Disorders
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the morphological and functional dynamics of dendritic spines, exploring their role in synaptic plasticity, learning, memory, and the implications of their alterations in neurodevelopmental disorders. The methodologies emphasize advanced imaging techniques, both automated and manual, to study spine structures and their molecular underpinnings.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, specifically the dynamics of dendritic spines in synaptic plasticity and their role in neurodevelopmental disorders.

topics.title: Molecular Mechanisms of HIV-1-Induced Neurotoxicity and Cognitive Impairment
topics.description: This research cluster explores the mechanisms of HIV-1-induced neurotoxicity, specifically through the effects of viral proteins like Tat and gp120 on neurons and glial cells. Key methodologies include the use of animal models and in vitro studies to investigate viral entry, chemokine receptor involvement, and neuroinflammatory pathways leading to synaptic injury and cognitive impairment.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, particularly the exploration of HIV-1 neurotoxicity and neuroinflammatory pathways in brain disease.

topics.title: Neurobiological Mechanisms of Vocal Learning and Maintenance in Songbirds
topics.description: This research cluster investigates the neural mechanisms and pathways involved in vocal learning, motor control, and auditory feedback in songbirds, with a focus on the anterior forebrain pathway and its role in song maintenance and neuroplasticity.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, specifically method development and application of specific methodologies to study the neural and behavioral aspects of vocal learning and song maintenance in songbirds.

topics.title: Epigenetic Regulation of Synaptic Plasticity and Memory Formation
topics.description: This cluster explores the role of epigenetic mechanisms, such as DNA methylation and histone acetylation, in synaptic plasticity and memory processes. It highlights how these modifications influence gene expression and contribute to neuroplasticity and cognitive function.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of epigenetic regulation in the context of learning and memory.

topics.title: Glutamate Metabolism and Transport Dynamics in Astrocytes: Mechanisms and Implications
topics.description: The research cluster focuses on the roles of astrocytes in glutamate metabolism, including the expression and function of glutamate transporters such as GLAST and GLT-1, in regulating glutamate clearance and synaptic plasticity as well as their involvement in preventing excitotoxicity through the glutamate-glutamine cycle.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of glutamate metabolism and astrocytic regulation in neurological health and disease.

topics.title: Innovations in Neuromorphic Computing: Efficient Algorithms and Memristive Hardware for Spiking Neural Networks
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the development and optimization of spiking neural networks (SNNs) using memristive devices and neuromorphic computing technologies. It explores bio-plausible learning rules, spike-time-dependent plasticity, and efficient training algorithms to enhance the energy efficiency and computational capabilities of SNNs, inspired by the dynamics of biological neurons.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on methodologies, emphasizing the development and application of innovative learning algorithms and hardware systems for neuromorphic computing and spiking neural networks.

topics.title: Understanding the Mechanisms and Consequences of Mild and Repetitive Traumatic Brain Injuries
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the study of mild traumatic brain injuries, particularly repetitive incidents and their long-term cognitive, behavioral, and neurodegenerative consequences. Methodologies include the use of advanced neuroimaging techniques, animal models, and assessment of brain network connectivity to investigate the underlying pathophysiological mechanisms.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, with an emphasis on studying the phenomena of repetitive and mild traumatic brain injuries and their sequelae.

topics.title: Neurotoxicity and Behavioral Impacts of Developmental Organophosphate Exposure
topics.description: This cluster investigates the neurobehavioral and cognitive effects of developmental exposure to organophosphate pesticides, with a focus on chlorpyrifos. Rodent models are used extensively to study the mechanistic pathways, emphasizing acetylcholinesterase inhibition and alternative pathways contributing to long-term neurotoxicity.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of neurotoxicity and behavioral impacts stemming from organophosphate exposure.

topics.title: Neuropeptide Regulation of Hypothalamic Gonadotropin-Releasing Hormone in the Reproductive Axis
topics.description: This cluster examines the role of neuropeptides such as kisspeptin in the regulation of gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) secretion from the hypothalamus, impacting the reproductive axis across different species. It highlights mechanistic insights into the neuroendocrine modulation of luteinizing hormone release via kisspeptin-GnRH interactions.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, as it consistently studies the neuropeptide regulation of specific reproductive functions and phenomena such as GnRH release and its modulation by various peptides like kisspeptin.

topics.title: Metal Ion Homeostasis and Neurodegeneration: Mechanisms and Protective Strategies
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the role of metal ion homeostasis, particularly iron, zinc, and copper, in neurodegenerative diseases. Key themes include the impact of metal dyshomeostasis on oxidative stress and neuronal damage, and the exploration of chelation therapy as a protective strategy against metal-induced neurotoxicity.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes related to the study of metal ions and their contribution to neurodegenerative processes.

topics.title: Advanced Techniques in EEG and MEG Analysis: Source Localization and Artifact Correction
topics.description: This cluster focuses on methodologies for EEG and MEG data analysis, with an emphasis on independent component analysis (ICA) for artifact removal, the use of advanced source localization techniques, and the integration of EEG with fMRI data to enhance spatial accuracy.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies.

topics.title: Neuronal Encoding and Processing in the Rodent Whisker System
topics.description: This cluster explores the neuronal encoding and processing of tactile information in the rodent whisker system, focusing on how mechanical inputs from whisker movements are transduced into neural signals. It examines both top-down cortical influences and the distinct roles of barrel and septal circuits in shaping sensory representations.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, particularly the study of the somatosensory system's response to whisker-based tactile inputs and the spatial and temporal integration of these signals within cortical and thalamic circuits.

topics.title: Advanced Behavioral Analysis and Tracking in Laboratory Rodents
topics.description: The research cluster explores high-throughput automated methods for behavioral analysis in rodent models, emphasizing the use of machine learning and computer vision technologies to enhance precision in tracking and quantifying behaviors. It underscores the importance of automation in improving the accuracy and reproducibility of complex mouse behavior studies conducted within home-cage environments.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on methodologies.

topics.title: Dynamics and Mechanisms of Synaptic Plasticity in Learning and Memory
topics.description: This research cluster investigates the synaptic mechanisms underpinning learning and memory, emphasizing the role of spike-timing-dependent plasticity, Hebbian learning, and homeostatic plasticity in shaping neural dynamics and enabling efficient information storage and retrieval in recurrent neural networks.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies.

topics.title: Neuronal Hyperexcitability and Genetic Overlaps in Migraine and Epilepsy Research Cluster
topics.description: This research cluster explores the shared mechanisms of hyperexcitability in migraine and epilepsy, highlighting the role of genetic mutations and ion channel dysfunctions. It emphasizes the use of antiepileptic drugs to manage symptoms and investigates the trigeminal system's involvement in pain processing.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of neuronal hyperexcitability and shared genetic pathways in migraine and epilepsy.

topics.title: Molecular Pathogenesis and Experimental Models of Spinocerebellar Ataxias
topics.description: The research cluster focuses on the molecular mechanisms, genetic underpinnings, and experimental models of spinocerebellar ataxias (SCAs), a group of neurodegenerative disorders characterized by CAG repeat expansions and progressive degeneration of the cerebellum and associated motor dysfunction. It emphasizes the pathological role of polyglutamine stretches in protein aggregation and neuronal degradation, exploring both cognitive and therapeutic aspects.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies used in research, such as genetic models and experimental approaches to study the disease mechanisms in spinocerebellar ataxias.

topics.title: Adaptive Plasticity and Reorganization of the Visual System: Mechanisms and Effects of Sensory Deprivation in Animal Models
topics.description: The cluster explores the neural mechanisms and outcomes of sensory deprivation, focusing on plasticity and reorganization in the visual cortex and pathways during critical periods. Studies highlight ocular dominance plasticity, the effects of monocular deprivation, and the potential for cross-modal compensation.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of neural plasticity and reorganization following sensory deprivation.

topics.title: Innovative Strategies for Blood-Brain Barrier Translocation and Neurological Disorder Therapeutics
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on novel methodologies for overcoming the blood-brain barrier (BBB) to enhance drug delivery for the treatment of neurological disorders. The central themes revolve around utilizing receptor-mediated transcytosis, developing advanced in vitro BBB models, and employing nanoparticles to improve CNS drug permeability and therapeutic efficacy.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies.

topics.title: Neurocognitive Mechanisms of Numerical Cognition: Insights from Symbolic and Nonsymbolic Processing
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the neural and cognitive mechanisms underlying numerical cognition, emphasizing the role of the parietal cortex and its functional networks in processing both symbolic and nonsymbolic numerical information. It highlights developmental aspects and conditions like dyscalculia, providing insights through advanced neuroimaging techniques.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies exploring functional brain networks and neuroimaging findings in numerical cognition.

topics.title: Genetic Variants and Pathogenic Mechanisms in Parkinson's Disease Focused on LRRK2
topics.description: The cluster explores the role of leucine-rich repeat kinase 2 (LRRK2) gene mutations in Parkinson's disease with an emphasis on genetic variants like G2019S and R1628P, analyzing their impact on neurodegeneration and expression levels. This includes the genetic and molecular mechanisms through which these mutations contribute to disease pathogenesis.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes surrounding genetic mutations and their effects on Parkinson's disease.

topics.title: Functional and Cellular Mechanisms of Taste Perception
topics.description: This research cluster explores the cellular and molecular mechanisms underpinning taste perception, focusing on the roles of specific receptors and taste bud cell types in processing various taste qualities, along with methodologies such as calcium imaging and neurophysiological recordings for understanding gustation.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies used to study taste.

topics.title: Transcriptomic Insights into Cell-Type Diversity and Neurodegeneration
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the application of transcriptomic profiling methods, primarily single-cell RNA sequencing, to study the molecular diversity of cell types in the brain and their changes in neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's disease. The methodologies involve advanced bioinformatics for analyzing high-dimensional single-cell data to characterize neuronal subtypes and uncover cellular mechanisms underlying neurodegeneration.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies used for transcriptomic profiling and analysis.

topics.title: Glioblastoma: Molecular Mechanisms, Cellular Targets, and Therapeutic Resistances
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the molecular and cellular mechanisms driving glioblastoma growth, including proliferation, invasion, and therapy resistance, exploring potential therapeutic targets like EGFR, CD133, and various cell signaling pathways.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Neuroimaging and Neural Correlates of Dyslexia: Insights into Reading Networks and Compensatory Mechanisms
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the investigation of neural correlates and cognitive processes associated with developmental dyslexia using various neuroimaging techniques. Studies reveal dyslexia-related abnormalities in phonological processing, connectivity of reading networks, and compensatory mechanisms within the language system.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of phonological processing deficits and neural network abnormalities in dyslexia.

topics.title: Neurocognitive Mechanisms and Rehabilitation of Visuospatial Neglect
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the phenomenon of visuospatial neglect, particularly following right hemisphere strokes, exploring spatial biases in tasks like line bisection and cancellation, and evaluating rehabilitation methodologies such as prism adaptation.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, particularly the study of spatial neglect and its underlying cognitive mechanisms.

topics.title: Pathogenesis and Strain Variation in Prion Diseases
topics.description: Research in this cluster focuses on the pathogenesis of prion diseases, characterizing the molecular distinctions between prion strains and understanding the conversion mechanisms of PrP(C) to PrP(Sc). It explores the implications of genetic and biochemical diversity in prions and examines their infectious properties across different species.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Advances in Functional Near-Infrared Spectroscopy for Cognitive and Neural Connectivity Research
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the use of functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) to investigate brain activity and connectivity across cognitive and motor tasks, emphasizing its applications in neuroimaging, non-invasive monitoring of brain function, and neuroergonomics.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies as it revolves around fNIRS as a method to study various brain functions and connectivity.

topics.title: Docosahexaenoic Acid in Neuroprotection and Cognitive Enhancement
topics.description: The research cluster focuses on the role of docosahexaenoic acid (DHA), an omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acid, in neuroprotection and cognitive enhancement, exploring its effects on brain development, inflammation, synaptic plasticity, and potential benefits in neurodegenerative conditions like Alzheimer's disease.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of DHA's impact on cognitive function and neuroprotection, exploring its biological roles in brain health and its potential therapeutic benefits.

topics.title: Mechanisms of Eyeblink Conditioning: Cerebellar Learning and Synaptic Plasticity
topics.description: This cluster explores the neural and synaptic mechanisms underlying eyeblink conditioning, highlighting the role of the cerebellum and its pathways, such as climbing fibers and Purkinje cells, in mediating learning and memory. Various conditioning paradigms, including trace conditioning, are employed to investigate the associative learning processes and synaptic changes involved.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, with an emphasis on the cerebellar circuits and synaptic mechanisms driving eyeblink conditioning.

topics.title: Neurotoxic and Neuroprotective Mechanisms of MDMA and Its Impact on Brain Function
topics.description: This research cluster explores the neurotoxic effects of MDMA, focusing on its impact on serotonergic and dopaminergic systems, and investigates both the acute and long-term consequences on neuronal function, including neuroinflammation, cognitive impairments, and protective adaptations.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of neurotoxicity and its functional consequences, specifically the study of MDMA-induced damage to brain systems and the exploration of potential protective strategies.

topics.title: The Impact of Thyroid Hormones and Vitamin D on Neurodevelopment and Neuroprotection
topics.description: This research cluster investigates the roles of thyroid hormones and vitamin D in neurodevelopment, particularly focusing on cognitive function, synaptic plasticity, and neuroprotection in conditions like Alzheimer's disease and multiple sclerosis. The methodologies commonly involve molecular analysis, animal modeling, and supplementation studies to understand their effects on axonal growth, inflammation, and synaptic signaling pathways.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, as it consistently explores the influence of specific hormonal pathways on neurodevelopmental and neurodegenerative processes.

topics.title: Neurophysiological and Genetic Insights into Movement Disorders: Dystonia and Tourette Syndrome
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the neurophysiological and genetic underpinnings of movement disorders, particularly dystonia and Tourette syndrome, highlighting alterations in cortico-striatal-thalamic-cortical circuits, sensorimotor integration, and the role of the cerebellum. The studies employ brain imaging and neurophysiological techniques to explore genetic influences and functional connectivity deficits.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, particularly in the application of brain imaging and neurophysiological techniques to understand genetic and circuit-level dysfunctions.

topics.title: Fragile X Syndrome: Molecular Mechanisms and Therapeutic Approaches
topics.description: This research cluster explores the pathophysiology and therapeutic strategies for Fragile X syndrome, focusing on the role of FMRP in synaptic function and plasticity. The studies leverage various animal models to understand the molecular interactions and consequences of FMR1 gene silencing, paving the way for potential treatments targeting mRNA translation processes.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.

topics.title: Neurocircuitry and Connectivity in Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder: Insights from Neuroimaging Studies
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on the neurobiological underpinnings of obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD), emphasizing the role of cortico-striato-thalamo-cortical circuits and their connectivity. It explores the alterations in functional connectivity and neurocircuitry using neuroimaging techniques such as functional magnetic resonance imaging to understand the cognitive and affective dysfunctions associated with the disorder.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, particularly the use of neuroimaging techniques to investigate the neural substrates and connectivity patterns in OCD.

topics.title: Aquaporin-4 and Its Role in Brain Edema and Fluid Homeostasis
topics.description: This research cluster examines the role of aquaporin-4 (AQP4) in brain edema and the regulation of water transport across cellular membranes, focusing on its expression in astrocytes and involvement in various brain pathologies and injuries.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of aquaporin-4's role in brain edema and fluid regulation.

topics.title: PET Radioligand Development and Application for Neuroreceptor Imaging
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the development and optimization of PET radioligands for imaging neurotransmitter systems in the brain, evaluating pharmacokinetics, and assessing receptor occupancy and binding potential through kinetic modeling and pharmacological challenges.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, particularly the development and application of PET radioligands for neuroreceptor imaging.

topics.title: Genetic and Molecular Studies of Neurodevelopmental Disorders: Insights from Mouse Models
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the genetic and molecular mechanisms underlying Rett syndrome, Down syndrome, and related neurodevelopmental disorders, utilizing mouse models to study the role of genes like MECP2 and DYRK1A and their impact on synaptic function and cognitive deficits.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, as it emphasizes gene studies, model creation, and analysis of molecular pathways.

topics.title: Evolutionary Dynamics of Mammalian Brain Structure and Function
topics.description: This research cluster explores the evolution of brain structure and function in mammals, focusing on the scaling of brain regions and their impact on cognitive capacities. The studies emphasize the comparative analysis of brain size, neuron numbers, and the evolution of complex behaviors across different mammalian orders.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of brain evolution and cognitive development.

topics.title: Consciousness and the Mind-Brain Interface: Integrative Approaches in Cognitive Neuroscience
topics.description: The cluster primarily explores the complex relationship between consciousness and the brain, focusing on neural correlates and theoretical frameworks that integrate interdisciplinary insights from cognitive science, neuroscience, and philosophy. It emphasizes the importance of information processing and network dynamics in understanding subjective experiences.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of understanding consciousness through interdisciplinary approaches and theoretical integration.

topics.title: Chemotherapy-Induced Cognitive Impairment: Mechanisms and Interventions
topics.description: This cluster of research focuses on the cognitive deficits, often referred to as 'chemobrain', induced by chemotherapy treatments, exploring underlying mechanisms such as neurotoxicity and hippocampal damage and investigating potential interventions using various animal models.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of understanding the phenomena of chemotherapy-induced cognitive impairment.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms and Pathophysiology of Micturition and Urinary Continence
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the neural pathways and regulatory mechanisms involved in micturition and urinary continence, highlighting both physiological and pathological conditions of the urinary bladder, urethra, and associated spinal and peripheral neural networks. The research spans from mechanistic studies of sensory and motor nerve pathways to potential neuromodulatory therapies for urinary dysfunctions.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes related to urinary physiology and dysfunctions.

topics.title: The Neural Basis of Creative Cognition: Brain Networks and Functional Connectivity
topics.description: This research cluster explores the neural underpinnings of creativity, focusing on the interaction of brain networks such as the default mode and executive networks, and the roles of functional connectivity and EEG alpha synchronization in creative cognition. The studies employ methodologies like fMRI and EEG to investigate divergent thinking and the neural dynamics involved in creative processes.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on methodologies.

topics.title: Meditation and Mindfulness: Neural Dynamics and Cognitive Enhancement
topics.description: This research cluster focuses on how meditation and mindfulness practices affect brain networks, particularly in relation to the default mode, salience, and executive control networks. The methodologies include functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI), electroencephalography (EEG), and various attention tasks to study cognitive performance enhancements and neural plasticity.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, which explore the application and effects of meditation practices on brain connectivity and cognitive functions.

topics.title: Neurophysiological Mechanisms of Error Processing and Performance Monitoring
topics.description: This research cluster examines the neural and cognitive mechanisms of error detection and performance monitoring, focusing on electrophysiological indices such as the error-related negativity (ERN) and error positivity (Pe). The studies explore the role of the anterior cingulate cortex in cognitive control, error awareness, and behavioral adjustments following errors, employing methodologies like EEG and ERP analyses within tasks such as the flanker task.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, particularly the use of electrophysiological measures to study error processing and cognitive control mechanisms.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms and Effects of Acupuncture on Brain Connectivity and Function
