topics.description: This research cluster explores the impact of acupuncture, including electroacupuncture, on brain connectivity and neuromodulation, using neuroimaging techniques like fMRI and EEG to investigate its effects on cognitive function, pain modulation, and neuroplasticity.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies, particularly the use of advanced neuroimaging and analysis techniques to study the brain's response to acupuncture.

topics.title: Exploring Non-Invasive Vagus Nerve Stimulation: Advances and Applications in Neuromodulation for Epilepsy and Depression
topics.description: This research cluster investigates the advancements in both invasive and non-invasive vagus nerve stimulation (VNS/tVNS) as therapeutic approaches for drug-resistant epilepsy and treatment-resistant depression, emphasizing the role of neuromodulation and highlighting the quest for optimizing stimulation parameters through both clinical and preclinical studies.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on methodologies, particularly on method development and consistent application of specific VNS and tVNS methodologies to enhance therapeutic efficacy for neurological disorders.

topics.title: Neurocognitive Mechanisms and Indicators: Exploring Pupillometry and the Locus Coeruleus-Norepinephrine System
topics.description: This research cluster investigates the role of the locus coeruleus-norepinephrine system in cognitive processes, using pupillometry as a non-invasive measure to explore cognitive load, attention dynamics, and arousal-related neuromodulation in both health and disease contexts.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, specifically studying cognitive processes associated with the locus coeruleus-norepinephrine system and its indicators, such as pupillometry.

topics.title: Neurobiological Mechanisms and Structural Biotypes in Antisocial Behavior and Psychopathy
topics.description: This cluster explores the neural underpinnings of antisocial behavior and psychopathy, focusing on the structural and functional anomalies in brain regions such as the frontal cortex and amygdala. Studies frequently investigate the roles of callous-unemotional traits, reward processing, and moral decision-making in understanding heterogeneous developmental trajectories of antisocial personalities.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the methodologies exploring neurobiological and structural brain correlates related to antisocial behavior and psychopathy.

topics.title: Neurological and Neuropsychiatric Manifestations of COVID-19: Mechanisms and Implications
topics.description: This research cluster examines the pathophysiology and potential long-term effects of SARS-CoV-2 on the nervous system, focusing on mechanisms such as neuroinvasion through olfactory pathways, neuroinflammation, and the role of ACE2 receptors in neuronal damage. It explores the clinical presentation of neurological symptoms in COVID-19 patients, including cognitive impairments and neurodegenerative risks.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, specifically the neurological impacts and mechanisms related to COVID-19.

topics.title: The Cognitive and Neural Mechanisms of Synaesthesia: Exploring Cross-Modal Interactions in the Brain
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the exploration of synaesthesia as a multisensory and cross-modal phenomenon where stimulation in one modality leads to experiences in another. Key studies investigate mechanisms underlying grapheme-color synesthesia, along with the bidirectional nature of synesthetic interactions, using neuroimaging and behavioral methodologies.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes of synaesthesia as it pertains to specific perceptual experiences and cognitive processes, such as automatic binding and cross-modal interactions.

topics.title: Neurodegenerative Disease Communication Through Exosomes: Roles in Pathogenesis and Therapeutic Potential
topics.description: This cluster highlights the role of exosomes and extracellular vesicles in facilitating intercellular communication within the nervous system, focusing on their involvement in neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. It explores their potential as biomarkers and carriers for therapeutic drug delivery across the blood-brain barrier.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes, particularly the role of exosomes in disease mechanisms and therapeutic applications.

topics.title: Impact of Electromagnetic Fields from Mobile Devices on Brain Function and Neurological Health
topics.description: The cluster primarily focuses on the thematic investigation into the effects of electromagnetic fields, particularly those emitted by mobile devices, on brain function, memory, and cognitive processes, as well as the potential neurological health implications observed in animal models.
topics.focus: The main focus of the cluster is on the themes.
